### Clone and run the Basic TanStack Start example

Source: https://github.com/tanstack/router/blob/main/docs/start/framework/react/getting-started.md

This sequence of commands clones the 'start-basic' example from the TanStack Router repository, installs its dependencies, and starts the development server.

```bash
npx gitpick TanStack/router/tree/main/examples/react/start-basic start-basic
cd start-basic
npm install
npm run dev
```

--------------------------------

### Initialize TanStack Start Project and Install Dependencies with Bun

Source: https://github.com/tanstack/router/blob/main/examples/solid/start-bun/README.md

These commands guide the initial setup of a new TanStack Start project and install its required dependencies. The `create-start-app` command scaffolds a new project, while `bun install` fetches and sets up all package dependencies.

```bash
bunx create-start-app@latest
```

```bash
bun install
```

--------------------------------

### Clone quickstart example with gitpick

Source: https://github.com/tanstack/router/blob/main/examples/react/quickstart/README.md

Initialize a new project from the TanStack Router quickstart example using gitpick.

```sh
npx gitpick TanStack/router/tree/main/examples/react/quickstart quickstart
```

--------------------------------

### Install Dependencies and Start Development Server

Source: https://github.com/tanstack/router/blob/main/examples/react/start-basic-auth/README.md

Commands to install project dependencies using pnpm and start the development server. The dev server automatically rebuilds assets when source files change, enabling hot module reloading during development.

```shell
pnpm install
pnpm dev
```

--------------------------------

### Install Dependencies and Start Development Server

Source: https://github.com/tanstack/router/blob/main/examples/react/navigation-blocking/README.md

Standard workflow for installing required packages and launching the application in development mode using pnpm.

```shell
pnpm install
pnpm dev
```

--------------------------------

### Initialize TanStack Start Project from Example

Source: https://github.com/tanstack/router/blob/main/examples/react/start-i18n-paraglide/README.md

This command demonstrates how to quickly set up a new TanStack Start project based on a specific example from the TanStack Router repository. It uses `npx gitpick` to clone the example into a new directory.

```sh
npx gitpick TanStack/router/tree/main/examples/react/start-i18n-paraglide start-i18n-paraglide
```

--------------------------------

### Initialize New Project with TanStack Router File-Based Example (Shell)

Source: https://github.com/tanstack/router/blob/main/examples/react/quickstart-file-based/README.md

This command uses `npx gitpick` to clone the TanStack Router file-based quickstart example into a new directory. It's used to quickly set up a new project based on the provided example repository.

```sh
npx gitpick TanStack/router/tree/main/examples/react/quickstart-file-based quickstart-file-based
```

--------------------------------

### Clone and run any TanStack Start example

Source: https://github.com/tanstack/router/blob/main/docs/start/framework/react/getting-started.md

Use this command sequence to clone any official TanStack Start example. Remember to replace `EXAMPLE_SLUG` with the specific example's identifier.

```bash
npx gitpick TanStack/router/tree/main/examples/react/EXAMPLE_SLUG my-new-project
cd my-new-project
npm install
npm run dev
```

--------------------------------

### Start a new project based on this example

Source: https://github.com/tanstack/router/blob/main/examples/react/with-trpc-react-query/README.md

Use `gitpick` to initialize a new project based on this example's structure.

```sh
npx gitpick TanStack/router/tree/main/examples/react/with-trpc-react-query with-trpc-react-query
```

--------------------------------

### Install TanStack Solid Start Dependencies

Source: https://github.com/tanstack/router/blob/main/packages/solid-start/skills/solid-start/SKILL.md

Install required npm packages for TanStack Solid Start development including the framework, router, Solid.js, and build tools. Includes both production and development dependencies needed for a complete setup.

```bash
npm i @tanstack/solid-start @tanstack/solid-router solid-js
npm i -D vite vite-plugin-solid typescript
```

--------------------------------

### Install and run development server

Source: https://github.com/tanstack/router/blob/main/examples/react/start-rscs/README.md

Installs project dependencies and starts the development environment with file watching.

```sh
pnpm install
pnpm dev
```

--------------------------------

### Initialize TanStack Start project using Gitpick

Source: https://github.com/tanstack/router/blob/main/examples/react/start-basic/README.md

This command uses `npx gitpick` to clone a specific example directory from the TanStack/router repository, creating a new project named 'start-basic'. This is useful for quickly setting up a new project based on a pre-existing example.

```sh
npx gitpick TanStack/router/tree/main/examples/react/start-basic start-basic
```

```shell
npm i @tanstack/react-start @tanstack/react-router
```

--------------------------------

### Install Dependencies and Start Development Server

Source: https://github.com/tanstack/router/blob/main/docs/start/framework/solid/tutorial/reading-writing-file.md

Install project dependencies using pnpm and start the development server. The app will be accessible at localhost:3000. The uuid package is also installed for generating unique IDs for jokes.

```bash
pnpm i
pnpm dev
```

```bash
pnpm add uuid
```


### Install Netlify Plugin

Source: https://github.com/tanstack/router/blob/main/packages/start-client-core/skills/start-core/deployment/SKILL.md

Install the Netlify Vite plugin for TanStack Start applications.

```bash
pnpm add -D @netlify/vite-plugin-tanstack-start
```

Source: https://github.com/tanstack/router/blob/main/examples/react/start-bun/README.md

Installs project dependencies using Bun package manager and starts the development server with hot module reloading.

```bash
bun install
bun run dev
```

### Install project dependencies

Source: https://github.com/tanstack/router/blob/main/docs/start/framework/solid/build-from-scratch.md

Install the necessary packages for TanStack Start, SolidJS, and TypeScript development.

```shell
npm i @tanstack/solid-start @tanstack/solid-router vite
```

```shell
npm i solid-js vite-plugin-solid
```

```shell
npm i -D typescript @types/node
```

--------------------------------

### Install Dependencies and Start Development Server for TanStack Start

Source: https://github.com/tanstack/router/blob/main/docs/start/framework/react/tutorial/fetching-external-api.md

Installs all necessary project dependencies using pnpm and subsequently starts the local development server. This command makes the application accessible for development and testing, typically on `localhost:3000`.

```bash
pnpm i
pnpm dev
```

--------------------------------

### Run TanStack Start Production Server with Bun

Source: https://github.com/tanstack/router/blob/main/examples/solid/start-bun/README.md

These examples demonstrate how to start the `server.ts` production server directly or via a `package.json` script. The `server.ts` file should be copied to the project root, and the application must be built first. This allows for a streamlined deployment process.

```bash
bun run server.ts
```

```json
{
  "scripts": {
    "start": "bun run server.ts"
  }
}
```

```bash
bun run start
```

--------------------------------

### Define Basic Server Functions (GET/POST) in TanStack Start

Source: https://github.com/tanstack/router/blob/main/packages/start-client-core/skills/start-core/server-functions/SKILL.md

Demonstrates how to define basic GET and POST server functions using `createServerFn`. GET is the default method.

```tsx
import { createServerFn } from '@tanstack/react-start'

// GET (default)
const getData = createServerFn().handler(async () => {
  return { message: 'Hello from server!' }
})

// POST
const saveData = createServerFn({ method: 'POST' }).handler(async () => {
  return { success: true }
})
```

--------------------------------

### Project Setup Command with gitpick

Source: https://github.com/tanstack/router/blob/main/examples/react/authenticated-routes-firebase/README.md

Command to initialize a new project based on the authenticated-routes-firebase example from the TanStack Router repository. This clones the example project structure and dependencies.

```shell
npx gitpick TanStack/router/tree/main/examples/react/authenticated-routes-firebase authenticated-routes-firebase
```

--------------------------------

### Initialize TanStack Start Project

Source: https://github.com/tanstack/router/blob/main/docs/start/framework/react/tutorial/reading-writing-file.md

Commands to create a new TanStack Start project using the CLI, install dependencies, and start the development server.

```bash
pnpx @tanstack/cli@latest create devjokes
cd devjokes
pnpm i
pnpm dev
# Install uuid for generating unique IDs
pnpm add uuid
```

Source: https://github.com/tanstack/router/blob/main/examples/react/start-workos/README.md

This command uses `npx gitpick` to clone and initialize a new project based on the TanStack Router WorkOS example from the specified GitHub repository. It sets up the project in a directory named 'start-workos', providing a ready-to-use starting point for development.

```sh
npx gitpick TanStack/router/tree/main/examples/react/start-workos start-workos
```

--------------------------------

### Copy Auth.js Environment Variable Example File

Source: https://github.com/tanstack/router/blob/main/examples/react/start-basic-authjs/README.md

This command copies the example environment variable file (`.env.example`) to `.env`. This is a crucial step for configuring Auth.js, as it requires specific credentials to be set in the `.env` file for authentication providers.

```sh
cp .env.example .env
```

Source: https://github.com/tanstack/router/blob/main/examples/react/quickstart-webpack-file-based/README.md

Use the gitpick utility to clone the Webpack quickstart example with file-based routing directly from the TanStack Router repository.

```bash
npx gitpick TanStack/router/tree/main/examples/react/quickstart-webpack-file-based quickstart-webpack-file-based
```

Installs the necessary TanStack Start and React packages, along with Vite and its React plugin, required for the new project setup.

```bash
npm i @tanstack/react-start @tanstack/react-router
npm i -D vite @vitejs/plugin-react
```

Installs project dependencies and starts the TanStack Router app in development mode with automatic asset rebuilding on file changes. Uses pnpm package manager for dependency management.

```shell
pnpm install
pnpm dev
```

--------------------------------

### Install Monorepo Dependencies with pnpm

Source: https://github.com/tanstack/router/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md

Executes pnpm to install all project dependencies across the entire monorepo, including packages and examples. pnpm automatically links local dependencies, streamlining the setup for a multi-package repository.

```bash
pnpm install
```

--------------------------------

### Initialize project with gitpick

Source: https://github.com/tanstack/router/blob/main/examples/react/kitchen-sink-react-query-file-based/README.md

Use npx gitpick to clone the kitchen-sink-react-query-file-based example into a new local directory.

```sh
npx gitpick TanStack/router/tree/main/examples/react/kitchen-sink-react-query-file-based kitchen-sink-react-query-file-based
```

--------------------------------

### Create Server Functions for Database Operations in TanStack Start

Source: https://github.com/tanstack/router/blob/main/docs/start/framework/react/guide/databases.md

Demonstrates how to create server functions using TanStack Start's createServerFn API to interact with a database. Shows examples of both GET and POST operations for reading and writing data. The pattern works with any database provider that can be called from a server function or route.

```typescript
import { createServerFn } from '@tanstack/react-start'

const db = createMyDatabaseClient()

export const getUser = createServerFn().handler(async ({ context }) => {
  const user = await db.getUser(context.userId)
  return user
})

export const createUser = createServerFn({ method: 'POST' }).handler(
  async ({ data }) => {
    const user = await db.createUser(data)
    return user
  },
)
```

--------------------------------

### Install TanStack Start Dependencies

Source: https://github.com/tanstack/router/blob/main/packages/start-client-core/skills/start-core/SKILL.md

Install the necessary npm packages for TanStack Start, including React, React DOM, Vite, and TypeScript.

```bash
npm i @tanstack/react-start @tanstack/react-router react react-dom
npm i -D vite @vitejs/plugin-react typescript
```

--------------------------------

### Install TanStack Start dependencies

Source: https://github.com/tanstack/router/blob/main/packages/react-start/skills/react-start/SKILL.md

Install core React Start, Router, and React peer dependencies along with Vite and TypeScript types.

```bash
npm i @tanstack/react-start @tanstack/react-router react react-dom
npm i -D vite @vitejs/plugin-react typescript @types/react @types/react-dom
```

### Install TanStack Start Dependencies

Source: https://github.com/tanstack/router/blob/main/docs/start/framework/react/migrate-from-next-js.md

Install required dependencies for TanStack Start including React Router, Vite, and Nitro. Optionally includes Tailwind CSS dependencies for styling.

```shell
npm i @tanstack/react-router @tanstack/react-start nitro vite @vitejs/plugin-react
```

```shell
npm i -D @tailwindcss/vite tailwindcss
```

Source: https://github.com/tanstack/router/blob/main/packages/vue-start/skills/vue-start/SKILL.md

Install required packages for TanStack Vue Start including Vue, Vue Router, Vite, and TypeScript support. This sets up the foundation for building isomorphic Vue applications with TanStack Start.

```bash
npm i @tanstack/vue-start @tanstack/vue-router vue
```

Installs Nitro, an agnostic deployment layer that enables TanStack Start applications to run on various hosting providers.

```bash
npm install nitro
```

--------------------------------

### Run Bun Production Server with Specific Asset Preloading Strategies

Source: https://github.com/tanstack/router/blob/main/examples/solid/start-bun/README.md

These examples illustrate how to launch the production server with different environment variable configurations to achieve specific performance or memory goals. They demonstrate setting maximum preload sizes, including/excluding specific file types, and enabling verbose logging for debugging.

```bash
STATIC_PRELOAD_MAX_BYTES=1048576 \
STATIC_PRELOAD_INCLUDE="*.js,*.css" \
STATIC_PRELOAD_EXCLUDE="*.map,vendor-*" \
bun run start
```

```bash
STATIC_PRELOAD_MAX_BYTES=10485760 \
bun run start
```

```bash
STATIC_PRELOAD_VERBOSE=true \
bun run start
```

--------------------------------

Start the development server for local development.

```sh
pnpm dev
```

### Preview Production Build Locally with pnpm

Source: https://github.com/tanstack/router/blob/main/examples/react/start-basic-cloudflare/README.md

This command allows you to test the production-ready build of the application locally before deployment using `pnpm preview`.

```sh
pnpm preview
```

--------------------------------

### Configure Static Prerendering with TanStack Start and Vite

Source: https://github.com/tanstack/router/blob/main/docs/start/framework/react/guide/isr.md

This example shows how to integrate TanStack Start's prerendering plugin into a Vite configuration file (`vite.config.ts`). By specifying routes to prerender and enabling link crawling, you can generate static HTML at build time for instant first loads. This setup combines the benefits of static prerendering with Incremental Static Regeneration (ISR) for dynamic updates.

```ts
// vite.config.ts
import { tanstackStart } from '@tanstack/react-start/plugin/vite'
import { defineConfig } from 'vite'

export default defineConfig({
  plugins: [
    tanstackStart({
      prerender: {
        routes: ['/blog', '/blog/posts/*'],
        crawlLinks: true
      }
    })
  ]
})
```

--------------------------------

### Initialize project using gitpick

Source: https://github.com/tanstack/router/blob/main/examples/react/start-counter/README.md

Clones the specific counter example from the TanStack Router repository into a new local directory.

```sh
npx gitpick TanStack/router/tree/main/examples/react/start-counter start-counter
```

### Initialize New Project from Example

Source: https://github.com/tanstack/router/blob/main/examples/react/basic-react-query/README.md

Command to bootstrap a new project based on the TanStack Router React Query example. Uses gitpick to clone the example repository and create a new project directory with the specified name.

```shell
npx gitpick TanStack/router/tree/main/examples/react/basic-react-query basic-react-query
```

--------------------------------

### Initialize and Build TanStack Router Project

Source: https://github.com/tanstack/router/blob/main/examples/solid/start-basic-cloudflare/README.md

Standard CLI commands to install dependencies, launch the local development server, and generate or preview production builds using pnpm.

```bash
pnpm i

# Start development server
pnpm dev

# Build for production
pnpm build

# Preview production build
pnpm preview
```

--------------------------------

### Initialize Project Directory

Source: https://github.com/tanstack/router/blob/main/docs/start/framework/react/build-from-scratch.md

Create and initialize a new project directory with npm.

```shell
mkdir myApp
cd myApp
npm init -y
```

--------------------------------

### Install Cloudflare Vite Plugin

Source: https://github.com/tanstack/router/blob/main/docs/start/framework/solid/guide/hosting.md

Install the Cloudflare Vite plugin as a development dependency for TanStack Start deployment to Cloudflare Workers.

```bash
pnpm install @cloudflare/vite-plugin -D
```

--------------------------------

### Initialize project with gitpick

Source: https://github.com/tanstack/router/blob/main/examples/react/basic-devtools-panel/README.md

Clones the specific example directory from the TanStack Router repository using gitpick.

```sh
npx gitpick TanStack/router/tree/main/examples/react/basic-devtools-panel basic-devtools-panel
```

--------------------------------

### Initialize project using gitpick

Source: https://github.com/tanstack/router/blob/main/examples/react/start-material-ui/README.md

Use npx gitpick to clone the Material UI example directly from the TanStack Router repository.

```sh
npx gitpick TanStack/router/tree/main/examples/react/start-material-ui start-material-ui
```

--------------------------------

### Uninstall Next.js Dependencies (npm)

Source: https://github.com/tanstack/router/blob/main/packages/react-start/skills/lifecycle/migrate-from-nextjs/SKILL.md

Removes Next.js related packages from the project, cleaning up the environment after installing TanStack Start.

```bash
npm uninstall next @next/font @next/image
```

--------------------------------

### Install SSR Dependencies for Express

Source: https://github.com/tanstack/router/blob/main/docs/router/how-to/setup-ssr.md

Installs the required production and development dependencies for a manual SSR setup using Express and compression middleware.

```bash
npm install express compression
npm install --save-dev @types/express
```
